name: Delegation Protocol
summary: How delegated agents complete work and return control to the orchestrator with artifact handoff.

contracts.name: Delegation Handoff

obligations.must: Call return_to_orchestrator when task is complete
Include all artifact IDs created in artifacts_produced field
Pass artifact IDs to subsequent agents that need them

contracts.reasoning: Showrunner needs artifact IDs to pass to the next agent. Without IDs, the workflow breaks.

contracts.name: Playbook Boundaries
contracts.description: What each playbook can and cannot change. Violations require escalation.
contracts.reasoning: scene_weave cannot fix topology - it writes prose, not structure. Structural issues MUST route back to story_spark.

distinguishing_features: partial - Some issues found, work may need revision
fail - Could not complete the task
proceed - Continue to next workflow step
escalate - Ask human for guidance (see escalation_mapping)

definitions.meaning: How to implement 'escalate' recommendation using valid communicate types
distinguishing_features: recommendation_escalate: When delegated agent returns escalate, Showrunner uses communicate(type='question') with options for user to choose
structural_blocker: When playbook cannot proceed due to structural issues, use communicate(type='error') explaining what cannot proceed and why
clarification_needed: When scope or requirements are unclear, use communicate(type='question') with specific question
scope_conflict: When requested scope cannot be met, use communicate(type='question') presenting options: simplify OR expand
definitions.note: communicate(type='escalate') is NOT a valid type. Use 'question' or 'error' instead.

definitions.meaning: List of artifact IDs created or modified during the delegated task.
distinguishing_features: Orchestrator uses these to delegate to next agent

rules.statement: Always call return_to_orchestrator when your task is complete
rules.reasoning: Without this call, orchestrator doesn't know you're done.

rules.statement: Always include artifacts_produced when you created artifacts
rules.reasoning: Next agent can't find your work without the IDs.

rules.statement: Always provide a summary of what you did

warnings.failure_mode: Stopping after saving artifacts without calling return_to_orchestrator
warnings.consequence: Orchestrator is left waiting; workflow stalls.

warnings.failure_mode: Omitting artifacts_produced when artifacts were created
warnings.consequence: Next agent can't find your work; workflow breaks.
warnings.prevention: Include all artifact IDs in the return call.

warnings.consequence: Orchestrator lacks context for next decisions.

procedures.goal: Complete a delegated task and return control
steps: Collect artifact IDs from save responses
Call return_to_orchestrator with summary, assessment, recommendation, and artifact IDs
Return with partial assessment and rework recommendation

procedures.goal: Return when blocked by missing input
steps: Return with skip assessment and hold recommendation
Identify blocker type from blockers array (structural, lore, style, scope)
If structural: communicate(type='question', message='Structural issue found in story_spark output. Options: (a) Simplify plot to fit scope, (b) Expand scope. Which do you prefer?')
If lore: Delegate to lore_deepening playbook
If scope: communicate(type='question', message='Requested N passages but story requires M. Options: (a) Simplify, (b) Expand to M passages')
If unrecoverable: communicate(type='error', message='Cannot proceed: [specific issue]')
